caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses an inferior myocardial infarction.

evidence.span_text: 1. Inferior myocardial infarction.

caps.proposition_text: The patient can resume swimming a few laps.

evidence.span_text: - Medical Treatment: The patient is taking a low-dose aspirin, and will continue taking it for life or until he is instructed to discontinue. Continue Brilinta for at least 1 year. Continue Crestor 40 mg once a day. Continue Toprol 50 mg once a day. The patient can resume swimming a few laps. He will enroll in cardiac rehab to gain confidence in returning to exercising and for dietary education and counseling. Liver function studies and lipid panel ordered. Repeat echocardiogram ordered to be performed in 6 months.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses the right radial artery cardiac catheterization site is clean, dry, and intact.

evidence.span_text: - Examination: Right radial artery cardiac catheterization site is clean, dry, and intact. No hematoma. Palpable radial pulse.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician measures the ejection fraction at approximately 50%.

evidence.span_text: The ejection fraction, obtained while he was hospitalized, was measured at approximately 50%, which is in the low normal range.

caps.proposition_text: An echocardiogram will be performed in 6 months.
